translations.descricao: Obesity is a multifactorial chronic disease characterized by excessive accumulation of adipose tissue that can impair health. It is associated with comorbidities such as metabolic syndrome, hypertension, obstructive sleep apnea, diabetes in the elderly, metabolic hepatic steatosis, and vascular risk. In the Brazilian context, it represents a significant challenge for the health system.
translations.sus.indicacao: Anthropometric assessment for identification of overweight and obesity in primary care consultations, with emphasis on integrated management for chronic disease. Use of liraglutide indicated for grade III or II obesity with comorbidities, after failure in lifestyle changes.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults and adolescents with BMI ≥25 kg/m², prioritizing those with risk factors such as hypertension, diabetes, or dyslipidemia.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Universal screening for obesity in adults through anthropometric measures, with lifestyle interventions as first line; pharmacotherapy for clinical obesity (BMI ≥30 kg/m² or ≥27 kg/m² with comorbidities).
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Globally, the prevalence of obesity in adults is 13% (about 1 billion people in 2022). In Brazil, it is estimated at 22.4% among adults in 2021.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence of obesity varies from 1 to 2% in adult populations, with higher risk in individuals with initial overweight.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Obesity contributes to approximately 4.7 million annual deaths globally, mainly due to cardiovascular and metabolic diseases. In Brazil, it is associated with high morbidity and mortality from NCDs.
